mechanism: Antenna reflector failed to fully deploy after launch
outcome: Total constructive loss — satellite cannot provide commercial broadband service
relevance: Deployment mechanism failure, largest single satellite insurance loss in 2023

mechanism: Power subsystem anomaly detected shortly after reaching GEO
outcome: Partial capacity loss, under investigation
relevance: Power system failure on new-build satellite, battery/solar array failure mode

mechanism: Power system anomalies on multiple MEO broadband satellites
outcome: Multi-satellite power degradation, reduced capacity
relevance: Constellation-level power issue, multiple satellites from same manufacturer affected

mechanism: ESD from solar storm caused loss of attitude control, satellite drifted uncontrolled
outcome: Zombie satellite — drifted through GEO belt, eventually recovered after months
relevance: Space weather causing ESD, loss of command, risk to neighboring satellites

mechanism: Progressive solar array power degradation leading to premature end of life
outcome: Satellite retired years before design EOL due to insufficient power margin
relevance: Gradual power loss pattern, accelerated end of life

mechanism: Sudden drop in solar array power output
outcome: Partial capacity loss, continued operation with reduced power budget
relevance: Sudden power drop vs gradual degradation, partial loss claim

mechanism: Multiple Thales Spacebus NEO satellites experienced similar issues
outcome: Bus-level design issue affecting multiple operators
relevance: Manufacturer design defect affecting fleet, not individual satellite

mechanism: Particle impact on solar array causing ~40cm diameter affected area and power reduction
outcome: Continued operation with reduced power, ESA published impact analysis
relevance: Documented micrometeorite impact with measured power loss on operational satellite

mechanism: Solar arrays accumulated thousands of micrometeorite impacts over 8+ year service intervals
outcome: Solar arrays replaced during servicing missions SM2 (1997), SM3B (2002), SM4 (2009)
relevance: Best-documented case of cumulative MMOD damage on solar arrays, ESA detailed analysis
source: ESA Hubble solar array inspection reports

mechanism: 5.7 years in LEO, 34,000+ documented impacts, extensive material degradation from atomic oxygen and UV
outcome: Retrieved by Space Shuttle, comprehensive catalog of space environment effects on materials
relevance: Gold standard for MMOD flux calibration, atomic oxygen erosion rates, UV degradation data

mechanism: First Falcon Heavy commercial payload, initial deployment uncertainty
outcome: Successfully deployed and operational
relevance: Launch vehicle novelty risk, deployment uncertainty on new platform

mechanism: Proton rocket upper stage failure left satellite in wrong orbit
outcome: Constructive total loss — satellite could not reach intended GEO slot
relevance: Launch failure leading to total loss, orbit insertion failure

mechanism: Briz-M upper stage anomaly, satellite reached GEO using own propellant
outcome: Reached GEO but with reduced design life due to propellant expenditure
relevance: Partial loss from launch anomaly, reduced remaining life

mechanism: Oxidizer leak in chemical propulsion system
outcome: Total loss in 2019 after 3 years of degraded operation
relevance: Propulsion system failure leading to progressive degradation then total loss

mechanism: Complete sudden power failure, satellite went silent
outcome: Total loss — no communication recovered, likely electrical/bus failure
relevance: Sudden catastrophic failure with no warning, total loss event

mechanism: Ka-band antenna did not fully deploy
outcome: Partial capacity loss — Ka-band transponders unavailable
relevance: Deployment failure leading to partial loss, specific subsystem affected

mechanism: Constellation program cancelled after significant investment
outcome: Program cancelled, no satellites launched — business risk, not technical
relevance: Constellation business risk, pre-launch cancellation

mechanism: Collided with defunct Cosmos 2251 at ~11.7 km/s, both destroyed
outcome: Total loss, generated >2000 tracked debris fragments
relevance: First hypervelocity collision between two intact satellites, Kessler syndrome risk

mechanism: Russian DA-ASAT test destroyed satellite, generating 1500+ tracked debris
outcome: Debris cloud threatening ISS and other LEO assets for decades
relevance: ASAT-generated debris significantly increasing collision risk in 400-600km band

mechanism: Chinese ASAT test destroyed weather satellite, generating 3500+ tracked debris
outcome: Largest debris-generating event in history, debris still in orbit
relevance: Major contributor to LEO debris environment, affects ORDEM flux models

mechanism: Battery malfunction with risk of explosion, emergency deorbited to graveyard orbit
outcome: Satellite decommissioned early, moved to graveyard orbit as precaution
relevance: Battery failure leading to emergency deorbit, total loss before EOL

mechanism: Electric propulsion system underperformance during orbit raising
outcome: Delayed entry to service, reduced remaining propellant for stationkeeping
relevance: Electric propulsion reliability, extended orbit raising risk

mechanism: Progressive S-band antenna degradation across constellation
outcome: Constellation-wide capacity reduction, accelerated replacement program
relevance: Fleet-wide degradation pattern, constellation insurance challenge

mechanism: Advanced Baseline Imager loop heat pipe blockage, instrument overheating
outcome: Reduced imaging capability during certain hours, workaround procedures implemented
relevance: Thermal subsystem failure on critical weather satellite, design-level issue

mechanism: Ariane 5 upper stage anomaly, satellite injected into wrong orbit
outcome: Satellite used onboard propulsion to reach GEO, reduced design life by ~2 years
relevance: Successful recovery from launch anomaly at cost of reduced lifetime
